seoTitle: How to Add Multiple Domains to Google Workspace Free
metaDescription: Add multiple domains to Google Workspace for free using the Admin Console, covering primary domains, secondary domains, and domain aliases for one account.
intro: Managing several brands or business units under one Google Workspace account is possible without paying extra, as long as you stay on the same subscription. This guide explains the difference between primary domains, secondary domains, and domain aliases, and walks through verifying and connecting each type. It's built for entrepreneurs and IT admins who want one login managing email across multiple properties.

steps.heading: Understand domain types in Workspace
steps.body: Know the difference between a primary domain, secondary domains, and domain aliases before adding anything, since each behaves differently for email and login.

steps.heading: Add a domain in the Admin Console
steps.body: Use the Google Admin Console to add a new secondary domain or alias to your existing Workspace subscription without extra cost.

steps.heading: Verify domain ownership and set up DNS
steps.body: Complete domain verification and update the required DNS records so Google recognizes the new domain as connected to your account.

steps.heading: Manage email addresses across domains
steps.body: Set up user email addresses like user@domain1.com and user@domain2.com, and review routing and branding to avoid login confusion between accounts.

faq.question: Does adding a second domain to Google Workspace cost extra?
faq.answer: No, additional domains can be added for free as long as you're using the same Workspace subscription.

faq.question: What's the difference between a secondary domain and a domain alias?
faq.answer: A secondary domain functions more independently while a domain alias mirrors the primary domain's settings; both let you use multiple domain names for email.

faq.question: Do I need to verify each new domain?
faq.answer: Yes, domain verification and DNS setup are required before Google Workspace will recognize and activate a new domain.

faq.question: Can users get confused logging in with multiple domains?
faq.answer: It can happen, which is why following best practices for email routing and branding helps avoid login confusion.
